objective: Determine whether ten additional participant-distinct ChildLens development recordings improve the precision and cross-participant stability of the already frozen v2 German speech measurement instrument without changing its models, rules, metrics, or gates.

selection.source: the already bound 192-row ChildLens restricted release manifest and released timed annotations
selection.excluded_participants: all participants in the immutable v1 30-recording split, including development and locked
eligibility: one ChildLens video object available for approved selective copy
participant absent from every v1 split row
released timed annotation object is locally hash-verified
at least 60 seconds of coalesced released speech-like timing
recording duration and manifest display size are positive
selection.selection_order: iterative least-filled stratum; ties by sha256(release_binding|protocol_sha256|media_key); one recording per participant
selection.sample_rule: a deterministic contiguous 60-second range in the union of released speech-like timing; the union offset is sha256(protocol_sha256|speech-block|media_key) modulo available speech duration

acquisition.approved_purpose: existing noncommercial ChildLens action-language grounding and model-calibration project
acquisition.storage: existing owner-private local ChildLens quarantine only
acquisition.retained_derivative: bounded audio-video clip spanning the frozen released-speech sample with five-second edge margins

measurement_instrument.shared_segmentation_rules: identical to v2, including thresholds, released-annotation support, maximum duration, shared byte-identical ASR input, and plus-or-minus 250 ms perturbations

analysis.prior_eight_outcomes: reuse the immutable v2 restricted per-participant results; do not rerun or retune
analysis.uncertainty: participant-cluster percentile bootstrap, 10000 replicates, 90% interval, fixed seed 20260706
analysis.replication_safeguard: a combined pass is insufficient unless the new ten-participant cohort also meets all point-estimate, coverage, yield, and perturbation thresholds; bootstrap-lower thresholds apply to the combined cohort because precision is the stated reason for expansion
analysis.heterogeneity: report old-eight and new-ten point estimates separately; any direction reversal or new-ten failure blocks a locked recommendation
analysis.success_claim: measurement qualification only, never ASR accuracy, human validation, ground truth, or hypothesis support

stop_rules: Stop if fewer than ten participants remain after excluding every prior v1 participant.
Stop if governance, storage, authentication, exact remote metadata, or source-media validation fails; do not substitute another recording after selection.
Stop if any frozen instrument or prior binding hash fails.
Stop if either the new-ten replication safeguard or the combined-eighteen gate set fails.
Do not inspect or evaluate the v1 locked split.
Do not run real-vs-shuffled or time-shifted vision-language alignment under this protocol.
Do not introduce another ASR, VAD, embedding model, threshold, segmentation rule, or sample expansion.
A pass recommends only a separately authorized locked alignment evaluation.

privacy.public_output: aggregate evidence and cryptographic receipts only
